description: The data streams provided by a sensor, recorded on the sensor asset as an ordered multi-valued reference to data_stream entities.
probe_semantics: Deliver the ordered NAMES of the data_stream entities the subject sensor ASSET references (its multi-valued data_stream reference), or the empty value when none was recorded.

given: a sensor whose data streams are the streams named Soil-A and Soil-B, in that order
a second sensor with no data streams
then: sensor_data_stream of the first sensor == [Soil-A, Soil-B] in recorded order
sensor_data_stream of the second sensor == the empty value

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='bundle_field'; field declared in a PHP attribute plugin's buildFieldDefinitions, not a YAML file

SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal set the value via 'record_log with sensor_data_stream set' (the log-record shape the proposer defaults to). Corrected: the field lives on the sensor ASSET itself, set at asset creation via the sensor given-step write surface; no log is involved.

SHAPE DEVIATION (cardinality + value): the reference is MULTI-valued and ORDERED; the value is the ordered list of referenced data_stream NAMES (reproducible strings, the companion_plants house form) — raw per-run data_stream UUIDs could never reproduce across runs or ports and so could never score. VALIDATED LIVE (oracle asset/sensor + data_stream/basic, 2026-07-23): two streams referenced in order b,a read back in exactly that order; a sensor with no streams delivered data:[] — the empty value.

REFERENCE ENSURE: data_stream entities are ensured by NAME (find-or-create via POST /api/data_stream/basic, the _ensure_term form lifted to a content entity) at sensor-creation time and referenced by id — the fixture never carries a per-run UUID. VALIDATED LIVE: POST /api/data_stream/basic 201 with name-only attributes.

STANDING-POLICY DECISION (BIND-WITH-FEATURE): treated as BIND-WITH-FEATURE(sensor) under Duke's standing policy 'identity terms bind DURING their feature's port' (term-dispositions-2026-07-22.md) without a fresh per-term elicitation. Reversal condition: Duke may re-disposition at the next wave boundary; an unbound or retired term costs only its spec.
proposed by openai:gpt-5.6-terra (propose-terms); PROVISIONAL until first_pack_seal is filled by a real sealed recording
